scope: Pass B full-surface expansion. Chargify (Maxio Advanced Billing) publishes ~34 API controllers (customers, subscriptions, products/product_families/price_points, coupons, transactions, invoices, payment_profiles, components/component_price_points, events, statements, webhooks, billing portal, custom fields, referral codes, reason codes, sales commissions, subscription groups, offers, insights, sites, etc). This bundle now covers every practical GET list/detail resource that is a durable, independently-syncable business object AND every create/update/delete mutation the declarative dialect can express against Chargify's REST conventions (page/per_page list pagination, single-key resource-wrapped JSON, form-encoded writes). Genuinely out-of-scope surface is documented per-endpoint below with a specific reason, never a blanket bucket. Chargify remains read-only in legacy; the write actions below are new Pass B capability (metadata.json capabilities.write flips true), gated by risk/approval.

excluded.reason: single-customer read is redundant with the customers list stream, which already emits every field the detail endpoint returns; no additional data is reachable only via the detail path

Chargify customer deletion is a rare, irreversible admin action typically gated behind support/account-owner approval outside the API's normal write surface; not part of the representative create/update pair Pass B targets for this connector
single-subscription read is redundant with the subscriptions list stream, which already emits every field the detail endpoint returns

reactivation requires a Chargify-specific reactivation-options payload (preserve_balance/include_trial/individual_component_options) with grace-period and dunning-state edge cases beyond the create/update/cancel triad Pass B targets as the representative subscription-lifecycle write surface
permanently erases the subscription and its billing history (Chargify's own docs mark this test-mode-only and irreversible); far outside the durable-business-data write surface Pass B covers
single-product read is redundant with the products list stream
single-product-family read is redundant with the product_families list stream

product price points are a per-product nested sub-resource collection; fanning this out over every product id requires the fan_out dialect's request-based id source, whose own child listing (products.json) is itself paginated — a parent-pagination-aware fan-out is not yet modeled by this engine increment, and legacy never covered price points at all, so this is deferred rather than half-modeled against a single hardcoded product id
single-coupon read is redundant with the coupons list stream

coupon hard-delete is an irreversible admin action removing discount history visibility for every subscription that redeemed it; deactivating via update_coupon's end-date fields is the safer documented alternative Pass B exposes instead
single-transaction read is redundant with the transactions list stream

refunds carry direct financial-reversal side effects against a specific historical payment transaction and gateway; Pass B's breadth-first pass covers the core object CRUD surface, not payment-gateway-adjacent operational actions
single-invoice read is redundant with the invoices list stream, which emits the same invoice fields

manual invoice status transition (paid/voided/pending) bypasses Chargify's own automated dunning/collection lifecycle and is documented as a support/edge-case action, not a routine sync-driven mutation
returns a binary PDF document, not a syncable structured JSON business record
single-payment-profile read is redundant with the payment_profiles list stream

creating a payment profile carries raw card/bank credentials (PCI-DSS scope); Chargify's own docs steer integrators to Chargify.js/hosted tokenization instead of a plain server-side API create, which this dialect's declarative form-body write cannot safely represent
same PCI-scope card-data concern as create; updates to card details are steered through Chargify.js tokenization, not a plain declarative write
removes a subscriber's active payment method, a rare account-remediation action with billing-continuity risk; out of Pass B's representative-write scope
Chargify's components list endpoint is scoped per product-family (GET /product_families/{id}/components.json); the bare top-level path does not exist as a documented site-wide endpoint

ENGINE_GAP: this endpoint requires fanning out over every product_family id, but Chargify's list envelope wraps each element under a singular resource key ({"product_family": {"id": ...}}), and the engine's fan_out.ids_from.request.id_field does a single flat map[string]any key lookup on each extracted record (engine/read.go's fanOutIDsFromRequest: rec[spec.IDField]) with no dotted-path/nested traversal — id_field can select "id" but not "product_family.id", so no id ever resolves and every product family's components would silently be skipped. Not attempted as a broken fan_out; deferred until the engine either dotted-path-resolves id_field or a computed pre-projection step is added. See docs.md Known limits.
single-component read is redundant with the components list stream this bundle would otherwise cover

allocating/updating a component's quantity on one specific subscription is a narrow per-subscription usage-metering action, not a create/update of the durable component definition itself; Pass B covers component-definition CRUD only
single-statement read is redundant with the statements list stream
webhook endpoint configuration is connector-account setup/administration metadata, not a syncable business-data object; no legacy precedent for reading or writing it
same as the GET above — account-configuration action, not a business-data mutation
webhook delivery-log listing is operational/observability data (retry status of past event deliveries), not a durable business object this connector syncs
webhook-replay is an operational remediation action, not a business-data mutation
generates a short-lived hosted-page invite link/token for the self-service billing portal, not a durable business-data record
returns a dry-run billing preview computed on the fly, not a persisted record

custom-field/metafield SCHEMA definitions are account configuration metadata describing the shape of custom data, not the business-data records themselves (per-resource metadata VALUES are already exposed as ordinary fields on their owning stream wherever legacy modeled them)
a narrow cancellation-taxonomy configuration list with no legacy precedent; low sync value relative to the core billing objects Pass B prioritizes
site/account metadata (the operator's own Chargify site configuration), not customer-facing business data
sales-commission reporting requires a separate elevated commissions-module entitlement not every Chargify site has enabled
subscription-group (family-plan) bundling is a narrower, newer Chargify feature with no legacy precedent; deferred past this Pass B pass in favor of the core object surface
pre-aggregated dashboard/reporting metrics computed server-side for the Chargify UI, not a raw syncable business-data collection
